content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed five amendments against the harden-create-from-bead guard in src/cli/project.rs and its supporting fs helpers. All five identify real, verifiable issues: two pairs are closely related (legacy-row repair mismatch; incomplete orphan-state recognition) and one is a focused test-coverage gap for operator-facing remediation text. Accepting all five.

## Votes

- `fr-2-35348dc8`: Accept
  Verified real inconsistency: FsTaskRunLineageStore::active_task_runs_for_bead (src/adapters/fs.rs:5089-5108) collapses entries via read_task_runs without invoking Self::fail_legacy_runless_entries, while record_task_run_start (src/adapters/fs.rs:5144-5148) does repair legacy run_id: null non-terminal rows before duplicate detection. execute_create_from_bead calls the read-only helper at src/cli/project.rs:574-579, so an upgraded workspace carrying a pre-run_id legacy row will produce a false DuplicateActiveBead rejection even though the authoritative run start path would repair and proceed. Remediation hint (mirror legacy repair in the read-only path, or filter out run_id.is_none() non-terminal rows) is correct and low-risk.
- `fr-2-c7cff677`: Accept
  Verified defensive gap: reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation at src/cli/project.rs:594-607 only maps AppError::ProjectNotFound to the orphan-repair guidance. If the project directory exists but run.json is missing (or unparsable), FsRunSnapshotStore::read_run_snapshot at src/adapters/fs.rs:1983-2006 surfaces a raw CorruptRecord on the fall-through line 607, bypassing the helpful 'repair milestones/<id>/task-runs.ndjson / restore the project' remediation the feature is meant to offer. Half-deleted project states are a realistic orphan-class failure; routing them through the same remediation path matches the stated goal.
- `fr-2-6789ab0c`: Accept
  Same root defect as fr-2-35348dc8, validated independently: the lineage helper lacks the fail_legacy_runless_entries pass (src/adapters/fs.rs:4649-4661) that record_task_run_start applies at src/adapters/fs.rs:5145. The amendment correctly notes the operator-facing symptom — a confusing DuplicateActiveBead with synthesized 'started_at=<rfc3339>' existing_run_id (src/cli/project.rs:614-617), followed by a spontaneous success on the next attempt once run start repairs the legacy row. Non-deterministic 'retry and it works' behavior is a real correctness/UX defect worth fixing even if it overlaps fr-2-35348dc8 in remediation.
- `fr-2-5f639d89`: Accept
  Concrete extension of the orphan-handling gap with two real fall-through sources: FsProjectStore::read_project_record returns AppError::CorruptRecord with file 'projects/<id>/project.toml' when the directory exists but project.toml is missing (src/adapters/fs.rs:1512-1529), and FsRunSnapshotStore::read_run_snapshot returns AppError::CorruptRecord with file 'projects/<id>/run.json' when run.json is missing (src/adapters/fs.rs:2001-2004). Both are plausible residue states from a half-deleted project and both skip the orphan-repair guidance today. Pattern-matching these corrupt-record shapes and a unit test locking in the message are appropriate.
- `fr-2-0a9a0f86`: Accept
  Legitimate maintainability concern with concrete benefit: the RunStatus::Failed branch (src/cli/project.rs:620-625), RunStatus::Completed branch (626-631), and multi-row active-lineage arm (634-653) each emit operator-facing remediation text (resume command, task-runs.ndjson repair, comma-joined offender list) that is not exercised by the four integration tests added at tests/cli.rs:13427-13684. Locking these messages into unit tests is a cheap regression guard for operator guidance that is precisely what this task is trying to make actionable; low cost and clearly aligned with the hardening goal.
